task: #48 P9.C17: Wire winning Q4 redesign and final #27 benchmark gate

decision.reason: P9.C15 rejected the compact32 Q4T16 selected-dual WMMA consumer and P9.C16 rejected compact tile-list/no-padding and wider-column selected-MoE alternatives. There is no in-repo Q4 redesign that satisfies the continuation target, so wiring a runtime path would only add unaccepted code.
decision.task_27_action: leave #27 open/blocked; do not mark completed because the <=110 ms combined bucket acceptance target is still missed

carried_forward_final_gate.correctness_evidence: Adjacent Q8_0 dual/single, Q4_K selected, Q5_K/Q6_K selected, dispatch/routing, and replay helper bundle passed (143 tests); 512/128 graph bench final logits were finite with deterministic final token 220.

q4_redesign_evidence.evidence: all-layer Q4 replay best 59.395 ms vs raw 62.199 ms (-4.5%); selected-MoE total 92.478 ms vs 93.138 ms (-0.7%), far above the <=35-40 ms Q4 continuation target
optimistic model lowers Q4 only to 54.775 ms because padding is 13.45% and residual tails are 5.40% of compact rows
64x16 measured 61.868 ms (-0.5% vs raw); 64x32 regressed to 91.831 ms

validation.notes: No correctness or throughput benchmark was rerun because #48 selected no runtime implementation. The formal carried-forward #27 gate is the retained P9.C11 blocked artifact, with P9.C15/P9.C16 providing the no-wire evidence.

next_actions: Move any further Q4 selected-MoE redesign work to parent kernel R&D or a new design task with evidence before touching hipENGINE runtime dispatch.
Continue hipENGINE P9 execution on the active #51 decode-repack and #28 small-op blockers.
